folio.label: 193r
folio.l: But I ne kan nat , bulte it to the bren
As can the holy Doctour , Augustyn
Or Boyce , or the Bysshop~ Bradwardyn
Wheither æ goddis worthy , fore wetyngö
Streynetú me needly , for to doon a thyngö
Needly , clepe I , symple necessitee
Or ellis if free chois , be grauntid me
To doo æ same thyngö , or do it nougút
Thougú god forn woot it , or æ it was wrougút
Or if his wityngö , streynetú neuäe a del
But by necessite , condicionel
I wole nat han to done , of swicú matere
My tale is of the Cok , as ©e may heere
That took his cou~seil , of his wyf , w sorwe
To walken in the ©erd , vp oon æ morwe
That he had mette æ dreem , æ I ©ow tolde´
Wo~mens cou~seils , ben ful often colde
Wo~mannes counseil , brougút vs first to woo
And made Adam , fro paradys to goo
There as he was ful merie , & wel at ese
But for I not to whom , it mygút displese
If I counseil of wo~men , wolde blame
Passe ouer , for I seide it in my game
Rede auctoures , ther they trete of swicú matere
And what they seyn of wo~men , ©e moun heere
Theise ben the Cokkes wordes , & nat myne
I kan noon harm , on no wo~man deuyne
¢ Faire in the sond , to bathe myrily
Lith Pertelote , and hire sustres by
A geyn the Sonne , and Chauntecler so free
Sang meriere , than the Mermayde in the See
For Phisiologus , seitú sikerly
How æ they syngen wel , and merily
¢ And so bifel , as æ he cast his eye
Amongö the wortes , on a boterflye
He was war of this fox, æ lay ful lowe
No thyngö , ne liste úi~ tha~ne , for to crowe
But cried a noon , kok , Cok , & vp he stirte
As a a man æ was affraied , in his herte
For naturely , a beest desiretú flee
From his contrie , if æ he may it se
Thougú he neuäe erst , had seen it , w his eye
This Chauntecleer , whan he gan úi~ espye
